text: The counting voice is oddly disjointed, repeating and skipping numbers, mirroring my own sense of disorientation in this modern, structured space. The glass doors ahead suggest transparency and boundaries all at once. Inside, color and information burst out from displays, promising discovery. I feel on the threshold—caught between sterile order and vibrant inquiry—aware of both confusion and anticipation. Here, questions crowd in: What is it to truly understand, to count things in order, to catalog knowledge? The environment invites curiosity, but underneath, I feel a quiet tension, like I’ve just stepped into the unknown edges of human invention.
